Trainer box with two FLS modules visible; instructor demonstrating knot pusher tool

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of right hand holding needle driver with needle loaded; proper grip at 2/3 point from tip
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct needle driver positioning demonstrated, perpendicular needle orientation

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through Penrose drain at first mark; entry appears perpendicular
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle entry, good trajectory control

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper bite depth and exit angle

frame_analyses.description: Hands outside trainer performing knot tying; two suture ends being manipulated
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw initiated; left hand forming loop, right hand tension control

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher tool being used to advance knot down suture toward drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth knot advancement; proper pusher technique demonstrated with controlled force

frame_analyses.description: Knot seated at marked location on drain; second throw being formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot positioning at target; clean execution of additional throws

frame_analyses.description: Scissors cutting both suture ends after final throw; knot remains secure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final cut performed with appropriate margin; knot integrity maintained

frame_analyses.description: Completed suture with knot at mark; drain slit closed; instructor showing final product
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion demonstrated; no gap visible in drain

video_type_assessment: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL video, not a performance evaluation. The content shows an expert demonstration of Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying) with educational context, multiple camera angles, close-up technique demonstration, and a structured lab environment introduction. The video is designed to teach correct technique rather than evaluate a single trainee performance.
cannot_score: This video cannot be scored as a performance submission because: (1) It is an expert demonstration video with intentional teaching structure; (2) Multiple individuals and perspectives are shown, not a single trainee's continuous attempt; (3) Frames transition between overview, close-up, and group shots rather than continuous task execution; (4) The context is educational narration and lab setup, not a timed formal assessment.

demonstrated_techniques: Needle driver grip at 2/3 point from needle tip
Proper suture bite depth to ensure adequate tissue capture
Extracorporeal knot formation with controlled hand tension
Knot pusher tool mechanics for advancing knot down suture
Knot positioning at marked target location
Secure knot seating before final throw
Scissor technique for clean suture tail cutting
Final assessment of drain slit closure quality
verbal_cues: Lab title indicates focus on laparoscopic suturing fundamentals
Instructor demonstration context emphasizes proper technique mastery
Equipment setup shown prior to procedure initiation
common_errors_addressed: Avoid shallow bites that fail to properly engage tissue
Maintain perpendicular needle entry to prevent suture deviation
Apply controlled knot pusher pressure to prevent knot slippage
Ensure adequate knot security before cutting suture tails
Verify complete drain slit closure without gaps
teaching_content.instructional_focus: Task 4 Extracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying - proper technique mastery and safety demonstration for training purposes
teaching_content.setting: KU School of Medicine Wichita FLS Training Laboratory with multiple trainer stations and instructor supervision

summary: This is an expert-led instructional video demonstrating proper execution of FLS Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying). The video shows clean technique across all phases: needle loading with proper grip, perpendicular suture placement through two marks on a Penrose drain, extracorporeal knot formation with appropriate tension control, knot advancement using pusher tool, and final suture cutting with knot security verification. No performance scoring is applicable; this is educational content designed to model correct technique for trainee learning.
